name: Old-California Empanaditas
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat butter, cream cheese, and salt together in a mixer until completely smooth and blended.
2. Work in flour to form a smooth dough. You can use a mixer for this step, but results are superior if you work the flour in with your fingertips, a fork, or a rubber spatula.
3. Flatten dough onto a sheet of foil to form an 8 x 6 inch rectangle. Chill overnight. You can chill it for several days before using.
4. Remove from the refrigerator 8 to 10 minutes before rolling out the dough. Many recipes suggest waiting 30 minutes, but it's easier to handle when as cold as possible.
5. Divide the dough into portions for easy rolling and to ensure the dough remains cold throughout.
6. Keep unrolled portion in the refrigerator until ready to use. Roll on a floured pastry cloth with a floured rolling pin or between two sheets of waxed paper. Loosening the waxed paper several times as you roll helps prevent sticking.
7. Shape as directed in specific recipes and chill before baking.
8. Brush the tops (only) of all pastries with egg yolk beaten with cream.
9. Bake in a moderate oven (350 degrees F) unless otherwise specified.
